items.title: Joyce misses out on heavyweight title shot as Gassiev opponent confirmed
items.summary: Murat Gassiev's first defence of his WBA heavyweight title will go ahead on Saturday after a replacement opponent was found for the Russian.

items.title: Brentford set to sign Wilson after West Ham exit
items.summary: Brentford are set to sign former England striker Callum Wilson on an initial 12-month contract after his exit from relegated West Ham.

items.title: Free agent John Stones convinced England’s epic 10-man win over Mexico proves Three Lions can win the World Cup
items.summary: JOHN STONES says England’s defence are pumped up by their brick-wall display at the Azteca - and ready to shackle old mucker Erling Haaland. Former Manchester City man Stones came off the bench to help the ten-man Three Lions defend their way to a 3-2 victory in a match for the ages against Mexico.

items.title: Henderson to miss rest of World Cup after freak injury
items.summary: England midfielder Jordan Henderson has been ruled out for the remainder of the World Cup.

items.title: James expected to return for Norway quarter-final
items.summary: England manager Thomas Tuchel expects Reece James to be available for the World Cup quarter-final against Norway on Saturday.

items.summary: CRISTIANO RONALDO is yet to secure World Cup success with Portugal - and the 2026 tournament could be his last chance to do so. items.title: France appeal yellow card to Fifa after extraordinary Donald Trump intervention over Balogun World Cup ban
items.summary: FRANCE have reportedly asked Fifa to VOID Michael Olise's yellow card - after USA striker Folarin Balogun was let off the hook. items.title: Trump boasts, Infantino hits back and Belgium vow action as Balogun furore rages on
items.summary: President Donald Trump says he asked FIFA boss Gianni Infantino to "review" Folarin Balogun's red card and World Cup ban, which has since been suspended, allowing the USA striker to play in the last-16 tie with Belgium.
